2606.20156.title: Modularity-Free Conflict-Averse Training for Generalized PINNs
2606.20156.paper_summary: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have become a powerful framework for solving PDEs by embedding physical laws into differentiable objectives. Despite their advances, training PINNs remains fragile: recent conflict-averse optimization schemes alleviate gradient interference between residual and boundary losses, but we show that their effectiveness deteriorates as model capacity increases. In this paper, we identify a capacity-induced failure mode, where overparameterized networks undergo functional modularity, self-partitioning into task-exclusive modules that suppress cross-objective interaction and hinder convergence toward Pareto-stationary points. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework, Modular-Sparsity Synchronization (ModSync), which integrates structural optimization into conflict-averse training by penalizing task-exclusive connections while preserving interaction-promoting pathways. Extensive experiments across diverse PDE benchmarks demonstrate that ModSync consistently prevents capacity-driven failures, sustains robust cross-objective coupling, and achieves state-of-the-art accuracy. Codes are available at \url{https://github.com/heejokong/ModSync}.
2606.20156.paper_summary_zh: 物理知識驅動的神經網絡（PINNs）已成為解決偏微分方程（PDEs）的強大框架，通過將物理法則嵌入可微分的目標中。儘管有了這些進展，訓練PINNs仍然非常脆弱：最近的抗衝突優化方案減輕了殘差損失和邊界損失之間的梯度干擾，但我們顯示，隨著模型容量的增加，它們的有效性會下降。在本文中，我們識別出一種由容量引起的失效模式，其中過參數化的網絡經歷功能模塊化，自我劃分為任務專屬的模塊，這抑制了跨目標的互動並妨礙了向Pareto穩定點的收斂。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的框架，模塊稀疏同步（ModSync），它通過懲罰任務專屬的連接，同時保留促進互動的通路，將結構優化整合到抗衝突訓練中。針對多種PDE基準的廣泛實驗表明，ModSync始終防止由容量驅動的失敗，維持穩健的跨目標耦合，並實現了最先進的準確性。代碼可在 \url{https://github.com/heejokong/ModSync} 獲得。
2606.20156.author: Heejo Kong et.al.
2606.20156.authors: Heejo Kong, Beomchul Park, Sung-Jin Kim, Seong-Whan Lee
